perturbation.original_text: The information that is intended to be covered by this agreement shall include, without limitation, any designs, concepts , ideas, technical, scientific, commercial information, know -how, trade secrets and processes, communicated to either party (“the receiving party”) by the other party (“the disclosing party”) or acquired by the receiving party from the disclosing party, du ring the course of the negotiations and discussions referred to in 1 as well as during the course of any appointment of the Company as contemplated in 1. All such information collectively shall be referred to in this agreement as the “confidential informa tion”.
perturbation.changed_text: The information that is intended to be covered by this agreement shall include any designs, concepts, ideas communicated to either party (“the receiving party”) by the other party (“the disclosing party”). All such information collectively shall be referred to in this agreement as the “confidential informa tion”.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'technical, scientific, commercial information, know-how, trade secrets and processes', the definition of 'confidential information' is significantly narrowed. This introduces an in-text contradiction because Section 3.2.3 refers to releasing a Measured Enterprise’s 'Confidential Information' to DTI/SANAS. Because the definition of 'confidential information' in Section 2 has been narrowed, it creates ambiguity whether releasing a Measured Enterprise’s 'Confidential Information' which could contain technical, scientific, commercial information, know-how, trade secrets and processes, now falls under the protection of this NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: BEE Online shall only provide information about its activities to SANAS or the DTI on request . The MD will acknowledge receipt of request by email within 2 days and will be responsible for the release of the information within 7 days. Before submitting a Measured Enterprise’s Confidential Information when requested by DTI, SANAS or required by Law, B EE Online shall notify the Measured Enterprise within 2 days of receiving the written request by email and will disclose the information within 7 days after Measured Enterprise has been notified BEE Online
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'confidential information' by omitting 'technical, scientific, commercial information, know-how, trade secrets and processes'. The contradicted text, Section 3.2.3, refers to releasing a Measured Enterprise’s 'Confidential Information' to DTI/SANAS. The modified definition introduces ambiguity as to whether the release of technical, scientific, commercial information, know-how, trade secrets and processes, which could be contained within the Measure Enterprise's Confidential Information, is now covered by the agreement. This creates a direct contradiction because the narrowed definition in the changed text makes it unclear whether the information that BEE Online is releasing to SANAS or the DTI under Section 3.2.3 is protected by the NDA, as the released information may no longer fall under the definition of 'confidential information' according to the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: BEE Online shall only provide information about its activities to SANAS or the DTI on request . The MD will acknowledge receipt of request by email within 2 days and will be responsible for the release of the information within 7 days. Before submitting a Measured Enterprise’s Confidential Information when requested by DTI, SANAS or required by Law, B EE Online shall notify the Measured Enterprise within 2 days of receiving the written request by email and will disclose the information within 7 days after Measured Enterprise has been notified BEE Online
perturbation.changed_text: BEE Online shall provide information about its activities to SANAS or the DTI on request. Before submitting a Measured Enterprise’s Confidential Information when requested by DTI, SANAS or required by Law, BEE Online shall notify the Measured Enterprise within 2 days of receiving the written request by email.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of 'The MD will acknowledge receipt of request by email within 2 days and will be responsible for the release of the information within 7 days' creates an in-text contradiction. This duty is removed, but the text directly following still references 'Before submitting a Measured Enterprise’s Confidential Information'. Without the initial timeframe for acknowledgment and release, the subsequent notification becomes unclear in its application.
perturbation.contradicted_text: BEE Online shall provide information about its activities to SANAS or the DTI on request. Before submitting a Measured Enterprise’s Confidential Information when requested by DTI, SANAS or required by Law, BEE Online shall notify the Measured Enterprise within 2 days of receiving the written request by email and will disclose the information within 7 days after Measured Enterprise has been notified BEE Online
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a clear timeline for acknowledgment and information release (MD acknowledges within 2 days, information released within 7). The changed text removes this timeline but retains the requirement to notify the Measured Enterprise before submitting confidential information. Because the timeline for acknowledging receipt of the request and releasing the information has been removed, this creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which implies this timeline exists. The redacted text creates ambiguity and an in-text contradiction as per the definition for Omissions – In Text Contradiction, which states that essential language removed or altered in one section creates a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: the parties will procure that their employees and agents to whom the confidential information is disclosed in terms of 3.2.2 are information of the confidential nature of the confidential information and prior to such disclosure, undertake to be bound by the terms of this agreement. Any breach of this confidentiality
perturbation.changed_text: the parties will procure that their employees and agents to whom the confidential information is disclosed in terms of 3.2.2 undertake to be bound by the terms of this agreement. Any breach of this confidentiality
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'are information of the confidential nature of the confidential information and prior to such disclosure' it creates an in-text contradiction because the employees/agents can't undertake to be bound by the terms of the agreement, if they aren't previously informed of the information confidential nature. Without understanding that it is confidential, any disclosure can't be considered a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: the parties will procure that their employees and agents to whom the confidential information is disclosed in terms of 3.2.2 undertake to be bound by the terms of this agreement. Any breach of this confidentiality
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement that employees and agents are informed of the confidential nature of the information before being bound by the agreement. This creates a contradiction because without this understanding, a breach of confidentiality is difficult to establish, conflicting with the contradicted text which assumes any breach is actionable. The explanation states 'Without understanding that it is confidential, any disclosure can't be considered a breach,' meaning there's a direct conflict between binding employees to confidentiality and ensuring they know what is confidential.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may request in writing at any time that any confidential information or any documents containing such confidential information, disclosed pursuant to the terms of this agreement and any copies thereof be returned with a written statement to the effect that upon such return the receiving party has not knowingly r etained in its possession or under its control, either directly or indirectly, any such confidential information or any documents containing such confidential information or any copies thereof and the receiving party shall comply with any such request with in seven days of receipt of such request.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may request in writing at any time that any confidential information disclosed pursuant to the terms of this agreement be returned and the receiving party shall comply with any such request within seven days of receipt of such request.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'or any documents containing such confidential information, and any copies thereof be returned with a written statement to the effect that upon such return the receiving party has not knowingly retained in its possession or under its control, either directly or indirectly, any such confidential information or any documents containing such confidential information or any copies thereof' creates an in-text contradiction. While the obligation to return 'confidential information' remains, there's no longer an explicit requirement to confirm the absence of copies, creating ambiguity on the extent of the return obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either party may request in writing at any time that any confidential information disclosed pursuant to the terms of this agreement be returned and the receiving party shall comply with any such request within seven days of receipt of such request.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies the return of confidential information, documents containing such information, and copies thereof, along with a written statement confirming the absence of retained copies. The changed text removes the obligation to return 'any documents containing such confidential information, and any copies thereof' and the related written statement. The contradicted text mirrors the changed text. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because while the obligation to return 'confidential information' remains, there's no longer an explicit requirement to confirm the absence of copies, creating ambiguity on the extent of the return obligation, as highlighted in the Perturbation Explanation. Therefore, the change directly contradicts the original intent and creates a potentially unenforceable term.

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree to protect the confidential information of the other party using not less than the same standard of care that would be applied to its own proprietary, secret or confidential information and that the confidential information shall be stored and disclosed in such a way as to prevent unauthorised disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree to protect the confidential information of the other party using the confidential information shall be stored and disclosed in such a way as to prevent unauthorised disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'not less than the same standard of care that would be applied to its own proprietary, secret or confidential information' the standard of care to protect confidential information becomes undefined, making the parties uncertain of their obligations and this creates an in-text contradiction. It is stated that the confidential information shall be stored and disclosed in such a way as to prevent unauthorized disclosure. Without an established standard of care, it's unclear how the 'unauthorized disclosure' will be prevented.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties agree to protect the confidential information of the other party using the confidential information shall be stored and disclosed in such a way as to prevent unauthorised disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text established a standard of care for protecting confidential information, which was 'not less than the same standard of care that would be applied to its own proprietary, secret or confidential information'. The changed text omits this standard, creating an undefined level of care. The contradicted text, being identical to the changed text, suffers from the same lack of a defined standard of care. According to the Perturbation Explanation, this omission makes the parties uncertain of their obligations and creates an in-text contradiction as it's unclear how the 'unauthorized disclosure' will be prevented without an established standard of care, as described by Omissions – In Text Contradiction.
